subject: Human Resources
title: Attracting Candidates
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Attracting Candidates.

hook.text: A company posts a job and gets 500 applications—but none are qualified. Attraction failed.

buildup.text: Attracting talent starts long before a job opens—it's about employer branding, reputation, and visibility.

discovery.text: Companies with strong employer brands get 50% more qualified applicants and spend less per hire.

twist.text: The best talent researches your company before applying. Glassdoor reviews matter more than your job ad.

climax.text: Attraction is marketing—you're selling the opportunity to work at your company to the people you want most.

punchline.text: Great companies attract. Struggling ones chase.

quiz.question: Why is employer branding important for hiring?
options: It attracts more qualified candidates at lower cost
It replaces the need for job postings
It only matters for tech companies
